bugs.description: The recursion base case only stops when len(b) == n, allowing b to grow beyond n and causing infinite recursion for cases where n < len(b) (e.g., n=1 with non-zero a). It should stop when len(b) >= n.

reviews.raw_response: {"verdict": "FAIL", "bugs": [{"description": "The recursion base case only stops when len(b) == n, allowing b to grow beyond n and causing infinite recursion for cases where n < len(b) (e.g., n=1 with non-zero a). It should stop when len(b) >= n.", "line": 15, "severity": "critical", "triggering_input": "1\n1\n5"}]}

bugs.description: The recursion base case only stops when len(b) == n, allowing b to grow beyond n and causing infinite recursion for cases where n < len(b) (e.g., n=1 with non-zero a). It should stop when len(b) >= n.

The backtracking algorithm explores an exponential number of candidate b sequences (up to ~2^{n-1} * (n-1)! nodes) without any memoization or pruning, leading to severe time‑limit risk for worst‑case inputs (n=10, no solution).

The backtracking algorithm explores an exponential number of candidate b sequences (up to ~2^{n-1} * (n-1)! nodes) without any memoization or pruning, leading to severe time‑limit risk for worst‑case inputs (n=10, no solution).

fixes.code:
import sys
from itertools import product

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    a_set = set(a)
    
    def differences(b):
        return set(x - y for x in b for y in b)
    
    def backtrack(b, visited_states):
        diffs = differences(b)
        if a_set <= diffs:
            return True
        if len(b) >= n:
            return False
        
        # Generate candidates
        uncovered = a_set - diffs
        # Pick one uncovered element, try to cover it
        target = next(iter(uncovered))
        candidates = set()
        for bk in b:
            candidates.add(target + bk)  # new - bk = target => new = target + bk
            candidates.add(bk - target)  # bk - new = target => new = bk - target
        
        for c in candidates:
            new_b = tuple(sorted(b + [c]))
            if new_b in visited_states:
                continue
            visited_states.add(new_b)
            if backtrack(list(new_b), visited_states):
                return True
        return False
    
    visited = set()
    initial = tuple(sorted([0]))
    visited.add(initial)
    print("YES" if backtrack([0], visited) else "NO")

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
from itertools import product

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    a_set = set(a)
    
    def differences(b):
        return set(x - y for x in b for y in b)
    
    def backtrack(b, visited_states):
        diffs = differences(b)
        if a_set <= diffs:
            return True
        if len(b) >= n:
            return False
        
        # Generate candidates
        uncovered = a_set - diffs
        # Pick one uncovered element, try to cover it
        target = next(iter(uncovered))
        candidates = set()
        for bk in b:
            candidates.add(target + bk)  # new - bk = target => new = target + bk
            candidates.add(bk - target)  # bk - new = target => new = bk - target
        
        for c in candidates:
            new_b = tuple(sorted(b + [c]))
            if new_b in visited_states:
                continue
            visited_states.add(new_b)
            if backtrack(list(new_b), visited_states):
                return True
        return False
    
    visited = set()
    initial = tuple(sorted([0]))
    visited.add(initial)
    print("YES" if backtrack([0], visited) else "NO")

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
